diagnosis.score_summary: The 0/100 alignment score indicates a total brand vacuum on the WWW channel. This is not a case of conflicting strategies; it's an abdication. The PDP has built a coherent, powerful brand narrative by itself, while the brand's own website remains a blank page, forcing the marketplace to define what the brand is.

truth_1.headline: Silence vs. Science
truth_1.gap_description: The WWW has no defined market position. The PDP, in contrast, has aggressively positioned the brand as 'The Scientific Miracle,' using clinical codes to justify a premium price. One channel is a ghost; the other is a high-tech lab.
truth_1.evidence: [POSITIONING] WWW: No Position vs. PDP: 'The Scientific Miracle' — '$13.99...#1 CLINICAL ANTIPERSPIRANT BRAND*...creates a fresh, clean, and trustworthy mood'
truth_1.why_matters: When your own site is silent on value, the marketplace sets the price and the story. You have ceded all control over your brand equity and pricing power to Amazon, which has successfully defined you as a premium, science-backed product without your input.
truth_1.the_hard_choice: Your brand's position is currently being managed by Amazon, and it's working. The hard choice is not between two strategies, but between action and inaction. Option A: Formally adopt the PDP's 'Scientific Miracle' positioning and build it across all channels. Option B: Create a new, untested positioning for your website and force the high-performing PDP to conform to it. You must decide whether to codify what the market has proven or to start over from scratch.

truth_2.headline: Mute vs. Mechanical Fix
truth_2.gap_description: The WWW communicates nothing. The PDP has a sharp, effective message as 'The Problem Solver,' focused on fixing the specific pain point of sweat. Your brand exists as a functional tool on Amazon and a silent object on your own site.
truth_2.evidence: [MESSAGING] WWW: No Message vs. PDP: 'The Problem Solver' — 'TRIPLE THREAT SWEAT PROTECTION: STRESS, HEAT & ACTIVITY'
truth_2.why_matters: A brand that only solves a problem on a marketplace shelf can't build loyalty. It's a utility, not a relationship. By failing to articulate a message on your own domain, you limit your brand to a single transaction and forfeit any chance of creating a deeper connection.
truth_2.the_hard_choice: The PDP has a clear message that converts by promising a direct solution. Your website has no message at all. Option A: Embrace the 'Problem Solver' identity, making it the core of your global communication strategy. Option B: Invent a different, likely more abstract, message for your website and hope it resonates better than the one already winning sales. You cannot A/B test a ghost.

truth_3.headline: Empty Shelf vs. Doctor's Note
truth_3.gap_description: The WWW offers no reasons to believe. The PDP provides powerful 'Derm-Validation,' using clinical authority and #1 status as its core proof points. Customers seeking reassurance on your site find an empty room, pushing them back to Amazon to buy.
truth_3.evidence: [KEY SELLING POINTS] WWW: No Proof vs. PDP: 'Derm-Validation' — '#1 CLINICAL ANTIPERSPIRANT BRAND*...100-HOUR DEFENSE...DERMATOLOGIST RECOMMENDED'
truth_3.why_matters: Trust is the currency of conversion. Your PDP builds trust with overwhelming proof, while your WWW undermines it with silence. You are actively training customers that the only place to get credible information about your product is on Amazon.
truth_3.the_hard_choice: Amazon is using the language of clinical authority to close sales with ruthless efficiency. You must now decide your source of truth. Option A: Codify this 'Derm-Validation' strategy, making it the bedrock of your brand's credibility everywhere. Option B: Ignore the data-driven proof that works and attempt to build a new set of KSPs based on an unproven narrative. Trust is built on evidence, not ambiguity.

truth_4.headline: No Identity vs. Smart Choice
truth_4.gap_description: The WWW offers the customer no identity to adopt. The PDP, however, frames the purchase as the choice of 'The Pragmatist'—a smart, rational decision-maker who picks the #1 market leader. Your brand is building a tribe on Amazon, while building nothing at home.
truth_4.evidence: [SELF-EXPRESSION] WWW: No Identity vs. PDP: 'The Pragmatist' — 'Best Sellers Rank: #1 in Antiperspirant Deodorant'
truth_4.why_matters: Great brands allow customers to express an identity. By default, your brand has become a badge of pragmatism and intelligence on Amazon. Failing to recognize and amplify this on your own channel is a missed opportunity to build a community around a shared value.
truth_4.the_hard_choice: Your Amazon page tells customers, 'Buying our product proves you're smart.' Your website says nothing. Option A: Accept that your customer is 'The Pragmatist' and build a brand experience that respects their intelligence and values effectiveness. Option B: Attempt to create a different, more aspirational identity on your website, risking a clash with the practical, results-oriented base you've already captured. You must decide if you're selling a tool or a totem.

truth_5.headline: Voiceless vs. Voice of God
truth_5.gap_description: The WWW is silent, projecting no personality. The PDP has adopted the unmistakable voice of 'The Clinical Authority'—serious, direct, and instructional. One channel is a void; the other is a consultation with an expert.
truth_5.evidence: [TONE OF VOICE] WWW: No Voice vs. PDP: 'The Clinical Authority' — 'Apply 2-3 clicks of product to underarms at bedtime...Stop use if rash or irritation occurs.'
truth_5.why_matters: A brand without a voice cannot build trust. The PDP's authoritative tone, while dry, creates a sense of safety and efficacy. The WWW's silence creates a sense of amateurism and neglect, actively damaging brand perception for any customer who visits.
truth_5.the_hard_choice: The marketplace has given your brand the powerful, trustworthy voice of an expert. You now have to decide if you want to own it. Option A: Adopt 'The Clinical Authority' tone globally and build your entire brand around expertise and efficacy. Option B: Develop a new, warmer voice for your website, creating a jarring personality split that confuses customers. You cannot be a doctor on Amazon and a casual friend on your own site.

marker_1.so_what: With no Purpose defined on WWW, the brand's entire reason for being is dictated by the PDP's 'The Healer' narrative. The brand home has abdicated its most fundamental role.

marker_2.so_what: The PDP defines the mission as 'The Democratizers,' making clinical strength accessible. The WWW defines nothing, ceding the brand's entire strategic intent to the marketplace.

marker_3.so_what: Amazon communicates a core value of 'Radical Transparency' through aggressive claims and clinical data. The brand's own site communicates no values, creating a moral vacuum.

marker_4.so_what: The PDP has successfully positioned the brand as a premium 'Scientific Miracle.' The WWW has no position, allowing Amazon to solely define the product's market value and status.

marker_5.so_what: A 'Reliable Expert' personality has been constructed on the PDP. With the WWW being silent, this de facto personality becomes the only one customers ever encounter.

marker_6.so_what: The PDP has a fully realized Sage/Hero personality. The WWW has none, creating a character vacuum that Amazon fills by default, defining the brand's soul.

marker_7.so_what: The PDP's 'Clinical Authority' voice is the only voice the brand has. The absence of a defined voice on the WWW makes the Amazon version the official canon.

marker_8.so_what: A strong 'Pharma-Code' visual language on the PDP defines the brand's look and feel. Without a competing vision on the WWW, this becomes the brand's entire visual world.

marker_9.so_what: The PDP's 'Problem Solver' messaging is sharp and effective. Since the WWW offers no alternative, this tactical messaging becomes the brand's entire strategic narrative.

marker_10.so_what: Amazon tells a 'Lab Breakthrough' story for the brand. The brand's own website tells no story, effectively outsourcing its own origin myth to a retailer.

marker_11.so_what: The PDP uses 'Derm-Validation' to build credibility. The WWW offers no reasons to believe, forcing customers to rely solely on Amazon for proof.

marker_12.so_what: The brand's unique selling proposition as a 'Tech Engineer' exists only on the PDP. The brand's own platform fails to communicate what makes it special.

marker_13.so_what: A 'Hard Product Sell' focus on the PDP drives conversions. The WWW has no focus, meaning the brand's only defined purpose is to be sold on Amazon.

marker_14.so_what: The core functional benefit of 'Protection' is clearly articulated on the PDP, but not on the WWW. The brand is failing to communicate its most basic job.

marker_15.so_what: The PDP promises the emotional benefit of 'Confidence.' The WWW promises nothing, leaving the brand's emotional connection with customers entirely in Amazon's hands.

marker_16.so_what: On the PDP, buying the product is an act of 'The Pragmatist.' On the WWW, it's a meaningless act, stripping the purchase of any identity-building value.

marker_17.so_what: The PDP frames the product as 'The Safe Choice' due to its market dominance. The WWW provides no social context, missing the chance to leverage powerful social proof.
